target.name: U.S. Artificial Intelligence Safety Institute
target.notes_excerpt: Housed within NIST. Develops science-based metrics, tools, and guidelines for AI safety evaluation. Established by Biden EO 14110 (Oct 2023). Paul Christiano served as Head of AI Safety. Runs AISIC consortium (290+ members). Renamed to Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) in 2025. Budget ~$10M (2024). target.name: National Institute of Standards and Technology
target.notes_excerpt: NIST develops voluntary AI standards and risk management frameworks for federal agencies and industry. Its AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0) is widely adopted. In 2025-26 its AI Safety Institute was renamed the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) under the Trump administration's AI Action Plan, with a mandate to revise the AI RMF.

source.notes_excerpt: Regina Barzilay is a School of Engineering Distinguished Professor of AI & Health in the Department of Computer Science and the AI Faculty Lead at MIT Jameel Clinic. She was diagnosed with breast cancer in 2014, which prompted her to conduct research in oncology. She develops machine learning methods for drug discovery and clinical AI, and her research has been recognized with the MacArthur Fellow

target.name: MIT Jameel Clinic
target.notes_excerpt: Abdul Latif Jameel Clinic for Machine Learning in Health, founded 2018 as a joint MIT–Community Jameel initiative. Conducts translational AI research across clinical AI, drug discovery, and epidemiology. Developed clinical AI tools MIRAI and SYBIL, and discovered antibiotics halicin and abaucin using AI-driven methods.

target.notes_excerpt: MIT's largest AI research lab, directed by Daniela Rus, covering robotics, systems, and machine learning. target.name: Apple
target.notes_excerpt: Consumer technology company deploying AI via Apple Intelligence across its device ecosystem. Pursues an on-device, privacy-first AI strategy using Private Cloud Compute for sensitive tasks. Partnered with OpenAI (ChatGPT integration) and Google (Gemini for Siri). Avoids public regulatory advocacy but leverages privacy standards as a competitive differentiator.

evidence: Li is a co-director of the Stanford Vision and Learning Lab

source.name: Fei-Fei Li
source.notes_excerpt: Fei-Fei Li is a Chinese-born American computer scientist best known for establishing ImageNet, the dataset that enabled rapid advances in computer vision in the 2010s. She is a professor of computer science at Stanford University, with research expertise in artificial intelligence, machine learning, deep learning, computer vision, and cognitive neuroscience. She has been called the godmother of AI

target.name: Stanford Vision and Learning Lab
target.notes_excerpt: Stanford Vision and Learning Lab (SVL), co-directed by Fei-Fei Li, focuses on computer vision, embodied AI, and robotics. Key projects include BEHAVIOR benchmark for household AI agents and the PAIR group for generalizable robot perception. Li has called for science-based AI governance.

evidence: Today, U.S. Senate Commerce Committee Chairman Ted Cruz (R-Texas) released a legislative framework designed to promote American leadership in artificial intelligence.

source.name: Ted Cruz
source.notes_excerpt: Ted Cruz serves as U.S. Senate Commerce Committee Chairman and released a legislative framework designed to promote American leadership in artificial intelligence on September 10, 2025. He introduced the SANDBOX Act, which creates a regulatory "sandbox" that gives AI developers space to test and launch new AI technologies without being held back by outdated or inflexible federal rules. Cruz is wei

target.name: U.S. Senate Commerce Committee
target.notes_excerpt: U.S. Senate committee with primary jurisdiction over AI, technology, and telecommunications. Chaired by Ted Cruz (R-TX) since 2025, who introduced the SANDBOX Act and a five-pillar light-touch AI framework. Oversees the Senate AI Working Group and has held hearings on U.S. AI competitiveness vs. China.
